Description: Courtyard by Marriott operates as hotel and offers lodging and hospitality services. It has 93 rooms and amenities include a meeting room, restaurants and lounges, indoor swimming pool, whirlpool, exercise room, and a golf club. The company is based in Dalton, Georgia. Courtyard by Marriott is a former subsidiary of LinGate Hospitality Group.

Name: Nintendo Of America Inc
Description: Good hand/eye coordination serves those who play Nintendo of America games well. Serving as the Western Hemisphere distribution headquarters for Japan's Nintendo, the company makes the #1 home game console, Wii (pronounced "we"), which is motion controlled and able to access Netflix. It also makes the #1 handheld game console, Nintendo DS, which features two screens and intuitive touch controls. On those consoles, users can access and play such classic games as "Mario Bros., Donkey Kong, PokÃƒÂ©mon," and Zelda. Nintendo of America is also well known for legacy systems, NES and Game Boy. Name: Scripps Health
Description: Care You Can Count On Whether you are searching for your next career opportunity or looking for care for yourself or a family member, youll find what you need at Scripps. Founded in 1924 by philanthropist Ellen Browning Scripps, Scripps is a non-profit integrated health care delivery system based in San Diego, Calif. We treat more than 700,000 patients annually through the dedication of 3,000 affiliated physicians and more than 15,000 employees among our five acute-care hospital campuses, hospice and home health care services, 27 outpatient centers and clinics, and hundreds of affiliated physician offices throughout the region. Medical Excellence Every Step of the Way Recognized as a leader in disease and injury prevention, diagnosis and treatment, Scripps has been ranked four times as one of the nations best health care systems by Truven Health Analytics. Our hospitals are consistently ranked by U.S. News & World Report among the nations best and Scripps is regularly recognized by Fortune, Working Mother magazine and The Advisory Board as one of the best places in the nation to work. Scripps is also at the forefront of clinical research, genomic medicine and wireless health care. With three highly respected graduate medical education programs, Scripps is a longstanding member of the Association of American Medical Colleges. More information can be found at www.scripps.org.

Name: Disney Cruise Lines
Description: Disney Cruise Line is a company that operates in the Leisure, Travel & Tourism industry. It employs 21-50 people and has $10M-$25M of revenue. The company is headquartered in Lake Buena Vista, Florida.

Name: Hudson's Bay Company
Description: Founded in 1670 as a fur trading enterprise, Hudson's Bay Company (HBC) is a Canadian retail conglomerate that owns department stores north and south of the US-Canada border. At home it operates Hudson's Bay, Canada's largest national branded department stores with 90 locations. It also has about 70 kitchen, bed and bath superstores under the Home Outfitters banner in Canada. In the US, HBC owns and operates the upscale Saks and Lord & Taylor department store chains. HBC also has its own credit card and financial services companies. After going public in 2012, HBC acquired US luxury retailer Saks in 2013 for $2.9 billion in its first purchase as a public company.

Name: Hulu
Description: Founded in 2007, Hulu is a premium streaming service offering live and on-demand TV and movies, with and without commercials, both in and outside the home. The company's offers a subscription video on demand service fully controlled and majority-owned by Walt Disney Direct-to-Consumer & International, a business segment of The Walt Disney Company, with Comcast's NBCUniversal as an equity stakeholder owning 33%. Hulu is based in Santa Monica, California.

Name: Orange SA
Description: Orange, Headquartered in Paris, France, is a French multinational telecommunications corporation and provides fixed-line and mobile voice and data services to consumers and commercial clients around the world. Services for corporate clients are provided by its Orange Business Services unit, which offers managed business networking and data services serving some 230 million customers in more than 30 countries. Headquartered in Paris, France.

Name: Acuity Mutual Insurance
Description: Acuity Mutual Insurance Company is a privately owned insurance services business with headquarters in Sheboygan, WI. Acuity has been in business since 1925. Some of the insurance coverage's they offer are auto, home, personal lines and and boats. They also offer the Rec-Pak which offers coverage for outdoor recreational equipment.

AAA is a not-for-profit member service organization; as of 2010, it had 51 million members. AAA of Western & Central New York provides services to its members, including roadside assistance and others. AAA's national headquarters are in Heathrow, Florida.

Name: HealthPartners
Description: HealthPartners is a not-for-profit HMO located in Bloomington, Minnesota. The organization offers health care, coverage, research, and education services to its members.

Name: Horace Mann Educators Corporation
Description: Horace Mann Educators Corporation (HMEC) is an insurance holding company. The Company through its subsidiaries markets and underwrites personal lines of property and casualty (primarily personal lines automobile and homeowners) insurance, retirement annuities (primarily tax-qualified products) and life insurance in the United States of America. HMECâ€™s principal insurance subsidiaries are Horace Mann Life Insurance Company (HMLIC), Horace Mann Insurance Company (HMIC), Horace Mann Property & Casualty Insurance Company (HMPCIC) and Teachers Insurance Company (TIC), each of which is an Illinois corporation, and Horace Mann Lloyds (HM Lloyds), an insurance company domiciled in Texas. The not-for-profit company provides managed care plans and related services to its members under the WPS Health Insurance and Arise Health Plan brands. Group, family, and individual offerings include HMO, PPO, and high-deductible plans. WPS also administers the policies of supplemental Medicare (Part A and Part B) members across the US, as well as western region TRICARE for Life (dental and pharmacy benefit) members. Subsidiary EPIC Life Insurance provides ancillary product including life, disability, dental, and vision plans, while WPS Community Bank offers banking and finance services in Wisconsin.

The UK's largest gas supplier, British Gas serves about 12 million homes and 1 million businesses with electricity, gas, and energy-related services in the UK. Through its Direct Energy unit, Centrica supplies gas and power to residential customers in Canada and the US. Centrica is also engaged in gas exploration and production and storage operations. Other activities include gas and electricity production, wholesale energy marketing, international retail energy marketing, drain cleaning services (the Dyno Group), and appliance sales.

BillingStreet: 5th Floor, Millennium Bridge House, 2 Lambeth Hill
Description: The name belies its demutualized status: Financial services group Old Mutual provides banking, insurance, and asset management services in about 30 nations in southern Africa, Europe, Asia, and the Americas. Founded in 1845, Old Mutual owns a majority stake of South Africa's Nedbank Group, which provides commercial banking, finance, investment banking, and other services. It also owns Old Mutual (US) Holdings, also known as Old Mutual Asset Management (US), or OMAM (US). Skandia Insurance offers insurance products and mutual funds, primarily in the UK and Sweden. Old Mutual has some Ã‚Â£262 billion (some $357 billion) in funds under management.

Name: UNILEVER PLC
Description: It takes two parents -- one Dutch and one British -- to make one Unilever. Unilever N.V. and Unilever PLC, together with their group companies, constitute a global food, personal care, and household products powerhouse. The group's vast portfolio of consumer products includes a dozen global brands, including Hellmann's (mayonnaise), Knorr (soups), Lipton (tea), and Dove and Lux (soaps), that each ring up more than Ã¢â€šÂ¬1 billion ($1.4 billion) in sales. Unilever's consumer goods are sold in more than 190 countries. The company was the world's #1 consumer products maker until Procter & Gamble purchased Gillette in 2005. Based in England, Unilever PLC trades on the London and New York stock exchanges.

Name: Marks and Spencer
Description: Marks & Spencer is an international, multi-channel retailer headquartered in London, United Kingdom. The company offers food, clothing and home products with a customer base of over 32 million people.

Name: G4S
Description: G4S is a market leader for providing global security and safety services, challenging Securitas for the #1 spot. Via subsidiaries and joint ventures, it provides security guards and bodyguards, runs prisons, and offers cash management and transportation to customers in some 125 countries. G4S also develops and installs security and electronic surveillance systems and offers safety consulting, training, and advisement services. Its US unit, G4S Secure Solutions (USA), specializes in armed guards and related security services for government and industrial clients. G4S was established in 2004.

Name: B&Q
Description: B&Q helps homeowners mind their p's and q's. The company sells some 40,000 home improvement products for the do-it-yourselfer (DIY) at about 355 retail stores throughout the UK and Ireland, including both large warehouses and smaller supercenters. It also does business in China through 40 stores, including the world's biggest B&Q in Beijing. Stores offer tools, lumber, insulation, and related products for building and repair projects, as well as items for home decor, such as flooring, hardware, paint, and wallpaper. B&Q also carries an array of home furniture and housewares, appliances, and gardening supplies. The company is a subsidiary of home improvement retailing giant Kingfisher.

Name: TUI Group
Description: The first step on a journey is likely to begin with TUI Travel. The group is the #1 or #2 source for leisure tour and travel services in many markets, as well as Europe's largest provider of online package holidays. It also distributes accommodations through international online wholesalers and its online travel agent in the UK, and increasingly Asia and Australia. TUI Travel runs some 3,500 retail travel stores across Europe, and operates with a fleet of 145 airplanes. In all, the group does business under some 200 name brands serving millions of consumers worldwide. TUI Travel formed from the 2007 merger of TUI AG's travel business with Britain's First Choice Holidays. TUI AG controls about 55% of TUI Travel.

Name: Care UK
BillingStreet: Connaught House, 850 The Crescent, Colchester Business Park
Description: As its name implies, Care UK is in the business of caring for children, adults, and seniors throughout the United Kingdom. The company's services range from primary care physician offices and walk-in clinics to nursing and residential homes for the elderly and mentally ill. It also provides support for the learning disabled and those with brain injuries. Though it is independently operated, Care UK works closely with public health providers, such as the NHS, to offer some primary care services. Though the general public makes up the majority of its client-base, Care UK also administers primary care to eight British prisons. Care UK is owned by Bridgepoint Capital.

Name: Sainsbury's
Description: J Sainsbury's trolley is filled with more than groceries. The UK's third-largest food retailer (after Tesco and ASDA) operates the Sainsbury's Supermarkets chain of some 570 stores throughout the UK. Its Sainsbury's online home delivery shopping service covers more than 90% of the UK population. In addition to supermarkets, it operates a fast-growing convenience store business with 440 shops under the Sainsbury's Local banner. The firm also owns half of Sainsbury's Bank (in a 50-50 joint venture with Lloyds Banking Group), and a pair of property joint ventures. Sainsbury also sells apparel and home goods, including cookware and bedding, in its supermarkets and online. In 2011 it acquired Global Media Vault.

Name: FIRSTGROUP PLC
Description: FirstGroup was not the first group to score a stake in the UK's deregulated bus and train industries, but it is one of the nation's largest diversified transportation enterprises. The company operates the First Capital Connect, First Great Western, First ScotRail, and First TransPennine Express railway franchises in the UK. Its trains carry more than 300 million people a year. FirstGroup provides public bus services that carry some 2.6 million riders a day throughout the UK. Its FirstGroup America unit provides school bus services (via First Student), operates public bus systems, and manages vehicle fleets in North America. Greyhound, North America's leading intercity bus company, is part of FirstGroup America.

Name: EE
Description: Hoping to fulfill the promise of its name (at least in the UK), Everything Everywhere (EE) provides mobile and fixed line services to 27 million customers across the country. A joint venture of Orange and Deutsche Telekom's subsidiary T-Mobile UK, it owns the Orange and T-Mobile brands in the UK (subscribers benefit from the ability to access either wireless network for better coverage). The company is the country's largest wireless provider and offers 2G coverage to 99% of the population and 3G coverage to 98%. It has also begun offering 4G service under the EE brand. It operates more than 700 retail stores through which it sells its services.

Name: Priory Group
Description: The Priory Group is one of the largest providers of mental health care services in the UK. The company manages some 70 facilities with about 3,400 beds and works in conjunction with the UK National Health Service. While its facilities treat a range of psychiatric and behavioral conditions through acute care hospitals, long-term and foster homes, neurological rehabilitation centers, and special education facilities, the company has become known as the celebrity choice for alcohol and drug rehabilitation. Private equity firm Advent International has agreed to acquire Priory Group from Royal Bank of Scotland.

Name: KIER GROUP PLC
Description: Kier Group provides a full range of construction and development services, primarily in the UK. Its largest segment is Kier Construction, which offers engineering and contracting services for a variety of building and infrastructure projects in the UK and overseas. Sectors served include government, health care, education, energy, and transportation. Kier Services provides management and maintenance services for public and private clients; it also operates a recycling facility in the West Midlands. Kier Property builds affordable and private houses, and develops and invests in office, industrial, and retail property across the UK. Kier Group has more than 50 offices across the UK.

Name: E.ON UK
Description: E.ON UK hasn't been a UK energy provider for eons, but long enough to become one of the top power suppliers and producers in the nation. A subsidiary of German utility E.ON, the company has operations in electricity generation and supply. E.ON UK supplies electricity (and natural gas) to 5 million nonregulated retail customers. The company has regulated power utility assets, and operates conventional and renewable energy-based power plants with a generating capacity of about 10,400 MW. To help E.ON UK comply with government clean air regulations, the company is developing major green energy projects, including the London Array and Rampion offshore wind farms.

Name: Thames Water
Description: Raindrops on roses -- and in reservoirs -- are some of the the favorite things of Thames Water. The company provides regulated water to 8.8 million customers and wastewater services to about 14 million people in London and the Thames Valley. It operates 100 water treatment plants, about 290 pumping stations, and 235 reservoirs. On the wastewater side of the business the company operates 66,500 miles of sewer lines and 350 wastewater treatment plants. In addition to its water and wastewater operations, Thames Water offers consulting, infrastructure, project management, engineering, and maintenance services. Subsidiary Thames Water Property Services manages 18,000 acres of properties at more than 5,000 sites.

Name: Co-op Insurance
Description: The Co-operative Group cares for its customers from the cradle to grave. The hodgepodge of businesses run by Britain's largest cooperative society includes farming, food and drug stores, travel agencies, banking, insurance, financial and legal services, and funerals. It's the UK's largest commercial farming operation and its fifth-largest grocery retailer with more than 2,800 food stores. Overall, it operates 4,800 outlets on high streets throughout the UK. Financial services include insurance, investment and retirement planning, as well as traditional and online banking. Finally, the Co-op is Britain's largest funeral service provider. Established in 1863, Co-operative Group boasts more than 7 million members.

Name: NEXT
Description: NEXT is a British multinational clothing, footwear, and home products retailer founded in 1864 and headquartered in Leicester, United Kingdom.

Name: SMITH & NEPHEW PLC
Description: Smith & Nephew can't make you hip, but it can make you a hip. Focusing on orthopedics, wound care, and endoscopy, the firm makes products to repair joints, skin, soft tissue, and bone. The company's orthopedics division makes hip, knee, and shoulder replacements, in addition to fixation and bone-growth devices used to repair broken bones. Smith & Nephew also makes arthroscopes, blades, digital cameras, and other minimally invasive surgical products through its endoscopy division. Its advanced wound management division manufactures and sells bandages and dressings used to treat a variety of injuries. The company operates in more than 90 countries worldwide.

Name: Thomas Cook
Description: Thomas Cook Group is a multinational travel service agency. The public company, Thomas Cook Group, went defunct in late 2019 from compulsory liquidation. Shortly after the brand was purchased, Thomas Cook operates as a website that books and helps establish traveling plans.

Name: Gas South
Description: Formed in 1938, Cobb Electric Membership Corporation, more commonly known as Cobb EMC, is a non-profit electric utility company serving parts of Cobb, Cherokee, Bartow, Paulding, and small sections of Fulton counties in Georgia. It also has a completely separate service area in southwest Georgia, serving Calhoun, Clay, Quitman, and Randolph counties.

Name: ResMed
Description: Founded in 1989, ResMed participates in the development, manufacturing, and marketing of innovative medical products for the treatment and management of respiratory disorders with a focus on sleep-disordered breathing. The company sells a comprehensive range of products worldwide through direct offices and a network of distributors. ResMed is headquartered in San Diego, California.

Name: SUNPOWER
Description: SunPower is all about being a stellar provider of solar. The company makes solar panels and systems under the SunPower, SunTile, and PowerGuard brands, among others. It sells these products to dealers, distributors, and system integrators for use by residential, commercial, government, and utility customers around the world. SunPower generates some 40% its sales from large-scale solar projects (solar farms for utility companies to offer alternative energy). While the company has offices across the globe, nearly 70 cents of every revenue dollar comes from customers in the Americas. SunPower is majority owned by France's  TOTAL SA.

Name: Virgin Money
Description: Virgin Money, a retail-only bank (and part of Richard Branson's  Virgin Group) is focused on providing residential mortgages, savings and credit cards. It also offers a range of investment and insurance products. Virgin Money serves 2.8 million customers through a range of channels, including online and mobile, intermediaries, call centers and a UK network of 75 stores. The company is seeking to leverage its strategic assets and capabilities to create a business with a demonstrable track record of growth, a high-quality balance sheet, and delivering a history of strong shareholder returns. The company went public in 2014.

Name: TSB
Description: TSB Bank is a retail and commercial bank that provides a wide range of financial products and services, including personal and business banking, mortgages, loans, savings accounts, insurance, credit cards, and online/mobile banking.

Name: ACN
Description: Founded in 1993, ACN is a direct seller of telecommunications, energy and essential services for residential and business customers. ACN provides phone service, high-speed internet, wireless, television, security and automation, computer support, payment processing and natural gas and electricity. ACN operates in 25 countries with offices located throughout North America, Europe, Latin America, Asia and the Pacific.  It is headquartered in Concord, North Carolina.

Name: Ericsson
Description: Ericsson is Swede on North America. The US subsidiary of Sweden-based global wireless network equipment leader Telefonaktiebolaget LM Ericsson oversees the North American business of its parent company. Its core network products are antennas, transmitters, switching systems, and other gear used to build wireless telecommunications networks. Ericsson Inc. primarily serves network operators, transportation companies, utilities providers, and broadcasters in the US. Its growing services business offers consulting, network build-out, and network management and maintenance services. The company also makes multimedia software and related products that enable such digital media services as Internet television.

Name: Lifetime Healthcare, Inc.
Description: Lifetime Healthcare wants good health to last a lifetime for New Yorkers. The company, which operates as The Lifetime Healthcare Companies, is a not-for-profit holding company that provides health insurance and delivers health care services to some 1.9 million members in New York State through its various subsidiaries. The company's core products are the health insurance plans offered through its Excellus BlueCross BlueShield subsidiary. Long-term care insurance is provided through the company's MedAmerica subsidiary. Primary and specialty care is provided to members via the company's Lifetime Health Medical Group. The company also provides third-party benefit administration services through its EBS-RMSCO subsi

Name: FortisBC Energy Inc.
Description: Fortis wields power. The group has holdings in 10 companies, primarily regulated electricity and gas distributors, serving about 2 million customers. Its Terasen unit is the major natural gas distribution utility in British Columbia and serves approximately 940,000 customers. Newfoundland Power utility distributes electricity to 240,000 people, while Maritime Electric serves customers on Prince Edward Island. FortisOntario serves parts of Ontario, and FortisAlberta distributes electricity to 480,000 customers in Alberta. Fortis also operates hydroelectric plants and hotels.

Name: The Trane Company
Description: TraneÂ®, a business of Ingersoll Rand - the world leader in creating and sustaining safe, comfortable and energy efficient environments, improves the performance of homes and buildings around the world. Trane solutions optimize indoor environments with a broad portfolio of energy-efficient heating, ventilating and air conditioning systems, building and contracting services, parts support and advanced controls for homes and commercial buildings.

Name: Government Employees Health Association Inc
Description: Founded in 1937 and headquartered in ees Summit, Missouri, GEHA is a self-insured, not-for-profit association providing medical and dental plans to federal employees and retirees and their families through the Federal Employees Health Benefits Program and the Federal Employees Dental and Vision Insurance Program.

Name: Liberty Utilities Co
Description: Liberty Utilities is a regulated water, wastewater, natural gas, electric and propane/air utility company providing local utility management, service and support to small and mid-sized communities across the United States.

Name: FULLBEAUTY Brands Inc
Description: Founded in 1901, Fullbeauty is a provider of swimsuits, apparel, accessories, and more. They are currently headquartered in New York City, New York.

Name: Young Living Essential Oils
Description: Young Living Essential Oils is a multi-level marketing company selling essential oils and other related products. The company was founded in 1994 and is headquartered in Lehi, Utah.

Name: National Australia Bank
Description: National Australia Bank provides a range of financial services including retail, business, and corporate banking, wealth management, insurance, superannuation, and various lending and investment products such as home loans, personal loans, and credit cards.

Name: The United Illuminating Company
Description: Headquartered in New Haven, Connecticut, UIL Holdings Corporation (NYSE:UIL) is a diversified energy delivery company serving more than 700,000 electric and natural gas utility customers in 66 communities across two states, with combined total assets of over $5 billion. UIL is the parent company of The United Illuminating Company (UI), The Southern Connecticut Gas Company (SCG), Connecticut Natural Gas Corporation (CNG), and The Berkshire Gas Company (Berkshire), each more than 100 years old. UI provides for the transmission and delivery of electricity and other energy related services for Connecticut's Greater New Haven and Bridgeport areas. SCG and CNG are natural gas distribution companies that serve customers in Connecticut, while Berkshire Gas serves natural gas customers in western Massachusetts. UIL employs more than 1,850 people in the New England region.

Name: Home Point Financial Corporation
Description: Home Point Financial is a national multi-channel mortgage originator and servicer. With the goal of providing a superior customer experience, Home Point has an innovative approach to lending, focusing on speed, quality and consistency for customers and partners.

Name: Toast Inc
Description: Toast, founded in 2011 and headquartered in Boston, Massachusetts, operates as a cloud-based restaurant software company. The Company designs and develops sale and management software system that helps restaurants to improve operations, increase sales, and create a better guest experience.

Name: Group Elite Communications
Description: Group Elite is the world's leading provider of Professional and Managed Services dedicated to the Installation, Support, Consulting and Sales of enterprise Workforce Optimization Solutions. Their award winning customer-centric approach is disrupting the industry and giving their customers the opportunity to fully exploit these complex solutions to take their contact centers to the next level.

Name: Flagship Credit Acceptance
Description: Founded in 2006, Flagship Credit Acceptance helps credit-challenged auto shoppers secure financing. The company's headquartered in Chadds Ford, Pennsylvania.

Name: Microsoft - Partner
Description: Microsoft (Nasdaq MSFT) develops, licenses, and supports software products, services, and devices worldwide. The company's Productivity and Business Processes segment offers Office 365 commercial products and services for businesses, including Office, Exchange, SharePoint, and Skype, as well as related Client Access Licenses (CALs); Office 365 consumer services, such as Skype, Outlook.com, and OneDrive; Dynamics business solutions, such as financial management, customer relationship management, supply chain management, and analytics applications for small and mid-size businesses, large organizations, and divisions of enterprises; and LinkedIn online professional network. Its Intelligent Cloud segment licenses server products and cloud services, such as SQL Server, Windows Server, Visual Studio, System Center, and related CALs, as well as Azure, a cloud platform with computing, networking, storage, database, and management services; and enterprise services, such as Premier Support and Microsoft Consulting that assist in developing, deploying, and managing Microsoft server and desktop solutions, as well as provide training and certification to developers and IT professionals on Microsoft products. The company's More Personal Computing segment comprises Windows OEM, volume, and other non-volume licensing of the Windows operating system, as well as patent licensing, Windows Embedded, MSN display advertising, and Windows Phone licensing system; devices, including Microsoft Surface, phones, and PC accessories; and search advertising, including Bing and Bing Ads. This segment also provides gaming platforms, including Xbox hardware, Xbox Live, video games, and third-party video games. The company markets and distributes its products through original equipment manufacturers (OEM), distributors, and resellers, as well as through online and Microsoft retail stores. Microsoft Corporation was founded in 1975 and is headquartered in Redmond, Washington.

Name: ConvergeOne (C1)
Description: ConvergeOne offers a comprehensive suite of collaboration and technology solutions, including unified communications, contact center services, cloud and networking solutions, and managed services to help businesses optimize their digital infrastructure and customer support.

Name: Enercare
Description: Established in 1848, Enercare is a provider of water heaters, water treatment, furnaces, air conditioners and other HVAC rental products, plumbing services, protection plans and related services. Enercare is headquartered in Markham, Ontario.

Name: Fidelity Investments Canada ULC
Description: Fidelity Investments Canada was established in Canada in 1987. Fidelity provides a full range of domestic, international and income-oriented mutual funds, as well as asset allocation, managed solutions, ETFs and a high net worth program to Canadian investors.

Name: Sunwing Vacations
Description: Founded in 2002, Sunwing s a Canadian low-cost airline company that offers scheduled and charter services from Canada and the United States to destinations within the United States, Mexico, the Caribbean, Central America, and South America. Sunwing is located in Canada.

Name: DoorDash
Description: DoorDash is a technology company that connects customers with their favorite local and national businesses in the United States and Canada. The company is headquartered in San Francisco, California.

Name: UnitedHealthcare Global
Description: UnitedHealthcare is dedicated to helping people live healthier lives and making the health system work better for everyone by simplifying the health care experience, meeting consumer health and wellness needs, and sustaining trusted relationships with care providers. In the United States, UnitedHealthcare offers the full spectrum of health benefit programs for individuals, employers, and Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries, and contracts directly with more than 1.2 million physicians and care professionals, and 6,500 hospitals and other care facilities nationwide. The company also provides health benefits and delivers care to people through owned and operated health care facilities in South America. UnitedHealthcare is one of the businesses of UnitedHealth Group (NYSE: UNH), a diversified health care company.

Name: Rural Community Insurance Company (RCIS)
Description: Founded in 1980, Rural Community Insurance Services is a company that specializes in providing crop insurance for farmers across the country.  They are based in Anoka, Minnesota.

Name: RelayHealth Corporation
Description: Launched in 2011, RelayHealth is McKesson Canada's web-based service that provides complete and secure information flow between patients and all of their health care providers. Focused on the patient point of care, RelayHealth accelerates information exchange, facilitates collaboration between health care providers, advances patient quality of care, and reduces overall health care costs.

Name: Warby Parker
Description: Warby Parker offers designer eyewear. The company designs glasses in-house to lower costs. Warby Parker partners with non-profits like Vision Spring to ensure that for every pair of glasses sold, a pair is donated to someone in need. Warby Parker is headquartered in New York, New York.

Name: Squarespace
Description: Founded in 2003, Squarespace provides software as a service for website building and hosting with pre-built website templates and drag and drop elements to create webpages. Squarespace is headquartered in New York, New York.

Name: Postmates
Description: Headquartered in San Francisco, California and founded in 2011, Postmates offers on-demand delivery from consumers favorite restaurants and operate a network of couriers who deliver those goods.

Name: HelloFresh USA
Description: Founded in 2011, HelloFresh USA is an online meal subscription company providing easy-to-use recipes with pre-proportioned ingredients. They are headquartered in New York, New York.

Name: Alliant Credit Union
Description: Founded in 1935, Alliant Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative headquartered in Chicago, Illinois. Alliant Credit Union offers Savings, Checking accounts, Credit Cards and more.

Name: Venmo
Description: At Venmo, were working to build a payment experience that's simple, delightful and connected. Since our founding in 2009, weve quickly grown from an idea hatched by two college roommates who wanted a better way to pay each other back to the go-to digital wallet thats known and loved by millions. What started off as a simple SMS platform to send and receive money has evolved into a social payments app allowing people to pay, split and share their experiences.

Name: Otter Products
Description: Founded in 1998, Otter Products produces protective products for smartphones and tablets through it's OtterBox, LifeProof, Liviri and OtterCares brands. Otter Products is headquartered in Fort Collins, Colorado.

Name: Donlen Corporation
Description: Donlen develops the fleet management industry's most innovative technology solutions and offers a proactive, hands-on approach to customer service. For more than 50 years, Donlen has empowered its customers to focus on their core business and drive continuous improvement in their fleet's operational and financial performance. Headquartered in Bannockburn, Ill., Donlen is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Hertz Global Holdings.

Name: WellDyneRx
Description: WellDyneRX is a Pharmacy Benefit Managers company that offers customized health plan designs, discount savings cards, and prescription and mail order pharmacy prescription services among other things.  They are based in Lakeland, Florida.

Name: Curo Financial Technologies
Description: CURO Group Holdings Corp. (NYSE:CURO), operating in the United States and Canada and powered by its fully integrated technology platform, is a market leader by revenues in providing short-term credit to underbanked customers. In 1997, the Company was founded in Riverside, California by three Wichita, Kansas childhood friends to meet the growing consumer need for short-term loans. Their success led to opening stores across the United States and expanding to offer online loans and financial services across two countries. Today, CURO combines market expertise with a fully integrated technology platform, omni-channel approach and advanced credit decisioning to provide an array of short-term credit products across all mediums. CURO operates under a number of brands including Speedy Cash, Rapid Cash, Cash Money, LendDirect, Avio Credit, Opt+, and Revolve Finance. With over 20 years of operating experience, CURO provides financial freedom to the underbanked.

Name: American First Finance
Description: American First Finance was founded to help consumers obtain financing to purchase the goods and services that they want and at the same time help merchants and service suppliers increase sales by providing that financing. American First Finance is headquartered in  Dallas, Texas.

Name: Winston Brands, Inc.
Description: Winston Brand, located in the Chicago Northwest suburb of Elk Grove Village, IL, the privately owned company maintains an entrepreneurial, fast-paced, yet casual environment with a constant focus on taking care of its customers. Winston Brand is one of the fastest growing direct to consumer multichannel merchandising companies recognized by the industry as a Top Cataloguer and Top Internet Retailer.

Name: i2c, Inc.
Description: Founded in 1987, I2C is a professional management and technology consulting services firm providing professional consulting, and training services to their clients in a complex business world.

Name: Kaiser Foundation Hospital
Description: Kaiser Foundation Hospitals provides health care services. The Hospital offers preventive care, immunizations, emergency care, screening diagnostics, and pharmacy services. Kaiser Foundation operates in the United States.

Name: TTEC Digital
Description: We believe that humanity and technology should co-exist, and that at the nexus of the most powerful experiences, humanity and technology collide.At TTEC Digital, we combine the expertise, innovation, partnerships, and passion of three industry-leading customer experience companies, bringing together TTEC, Avtex, and VoiceFoundry to help our clients find, win, and keep more customers.

Name: Genesis Financial Solutions Inc
Description: Founded in 2001, Genesis Financial Solutions is a company that provides consumer financing options for merchants and service providers. Genesis Financial Solutions is located in Oregon.

Name: PPL Electric Utilities
Description: PPL Electric Utilities provides electricity generation, transmission, and distribution services to customers in Pennsylvania. The company also offers energy efficiency programs, renewable energy solutions, and electric vehicle charging infrastructure.

Name: ZEISS Company
Description:
ZEISS is an internationally leading technology enterprise operating in the fields of optics and optoelectronics. The ZEISS Group develops, produces and distributes measuring technology, microscopes, medical technology, eyeglass lenses, camera and cinema lenses, binoculars and semiconductor manufacturing equipment. With its solutions, the company constantly advances the world of optics and helps shape technological progress. ZEISS is divided up into the four segments Industrial Quality & Research, Medical Technology, Consumer Markets and Semiconductor Manufacturing Technology. The ZEISS Group is represented in more than 40 countries and has over 50 sales and service locations, more than 30 manufacturing sites and about 25 research and development centers around the globe. 

In fiscal year 2016/17 the company generated revenue approximating â‚¬5.3 billion with around 27,000 employees. Founded in 1846 in Jena, the company is headquartered in Oberkochen, Germany. Carl Zeiss AG is the strategic management holding company that manages the ZEISS Group. The company is wholly owned by the Carl Zeiss Stiftung (Carl Zeiss Foundation).

Name: Ferguson
Description: Founded in 1953 and headquartered in Newport News, Virginia, Ferguson Enterprises is an U.S distributor of plumbing supplies, PVF, waterworks and fire and fabrication products. It is also a major distributor of HVAC equipment and industrial products and services.

Name: Acentra Health (formerly KEPRO)
Description: Founded in 1985, KEPRO is a CMS-designated quality improvement organization and care management organization. It offers solutions to reduce the utilization of health care resources and optimize the quality of care for public and commercial clients. KEPRO is headquartered in Pennsylvania, with offices in Alabama, Florida, Illinois, Maine, Maryland, Massachusetts, Minnesota, Ohio, Oregon, South Carolina, Tennessee, Virginia, and West Virginia.

Name: T-Mobile Polska
Description: T-Mobile Poland SA, founded in 1995 and located in Warsaw, Poland, is a mobile telecommunications provider offering wireless service, cell phones, and accessories.

Name: Amynta Group
Description: Amynta Group is an insurance company specializing in property, casualty and specialty insurance as well as warranty and protection. Amynta Group was founded in 2018 and is headquartered in New York.

Name: SDGE
Description: SDG&E is a public utility provider located in southwestern California. The company was originally founded in 1881 and is headquartered in San Diego, California. The company provides gas and electric energy services throughout San Diego and southern Orange counties in California. They are a subsidiary of Sempra Energy.

Name: Conair
Description: Conair is a developer, manufacturer and marketer of health and beauty products and kitchen and electronic appliances. The company was founded in 1959 and the headquarters is located in East Windsor, New Jersey. Brands under the Conair Corporation include Cuisinart, Waring Commercial, Scunci, Rusk, Argan Color, BaBylisPro, and Conair hair, health, grooming, and home products.

Name: LTCG
Description: LTCG was founded in 1992. This company provides  business process outsourcing for the insurance industry. Their headquarters are located in Eden Prairie, Minnesota.

Name: Propel Holdings
Description: Propel is an innovative, online financial technology ("fintech") company, committed to credit inclusion by providing fair, fast and transparent access to credit with exceptional service using its proprietary online lending platform. Through its operating brands, MoneyKey and CreditFresh, Propel is focused on providing access to credit to U.S. consumers who struggle to access credit from mainstream credit providers. Propel is headquartered in Toronto, Ontario.

Name: Seatgeek
Description: SeatGeek Inc. operates SeatGeek, a search engine and mobile-focused ticket marketplace that allows fans to buy and sell tickets for live events. The company SeatGeek search engine allows users to find tickets to local ticketed performances, such as concerts, sports, theater, Broadway, and comedy shows. The company was founded in 2009 and is based out of  New York City, New York.

Name: Casper
Description: Casper is a sleep startup that launched a mattress sold directly to consumers, eliminating commission driven, inflated prices. The sleep surface was developed in-house by a team of product engineers with experience from IDEO, has a sleek design, and is delivered right to your door in a box. Casper was founded in 2013 and headquarters are in New York City, New York.

Name: AllBirds
Description: AllBirds is a footwear company that uses a direct-to-consumer approach. Headquartered in 2015, AllBirds is based in San Francisco, California.

Name: Bosch US
Description: Bosch US is a company that specializes in the manufacturing of several products that can be used at home and in a vehicle. They are based in Farmington Hills, Michigan.

Name: Michael Kors
Description: Michael Kors is a world-renowned, award-winning designer of luxury accessories and ready-to-wear. His namesake company, established in 1981, currently produces a range of products under his signature Michael Kors Collection, MICHAEL Michael Kors and Michael Kors Mens labels. These products include accessories, footwear, watches, jewelry, womenâ€™s and menâ€™s ready-to-wear, wearable technology, eyewear and a full line of fragrance products.

Name: Opendoor
Description: Opendoor provides a way to buy, sell or trade-in a home. It is specialized in Real Estate, Technology, Data Science, Design, and Operations. The company was founded in 2014 and is based in San Francisco, California.

Name: Nokia
Description: Nokia Corporation provides network infrastructure, 5G solutions, cloud and IT services, IP and optical networking, software and analytics, IoT platforms, health solutions, and licensing.

Name: Nordstjernan
Description: Nordstjernan AB is an investment company that is engaged in industrial operations, financial investments, real estate, healthcare, consumer goods, technology, media, telecommunications, and environmental services.

Name: Loomis
Description: Loomis AB specializes in providing cash handling services, secure transportation, ATM services, cash management solutions, smart safe technology, and security consulting services to businesses and financial institutions worldwide.

Name: Danske Bank
Description: Provides a wide range of banking and financial services to individuals, businesses, and institutional clients.

Name: ICA Gruppen

Description:
Kino Tapiola on perinteinen yhden salin elokuvateatteri Espoo Tapiolassa. Se on yksi harvoista pääkaupunkiseudulla alkuperäisessä, kulttuurisuojellussa asussaan säilyneistä vanhan ajan teattereista. Kesällä 2010 valmistuneessa täysmittaisessa remontissa Kino Tapiolan konehuone uudistettiin viimeisimmän teknologian mukaiseksi ja siksi Kino Tapiolassa onkin nyt mahdollista käydä katsomassa vaikkapa uusimpia 3D-ensi-iltaelokuvia tai nauttia huippuoopperasta satelliittilähetyksenä suoraan Lontoon Covent Gardenista. Puhumattakaan siitä, mitä mahdollisuuksia tuo Kino Tapiolan tyylikäs 1950-luvun ulkoasu yhdistettynä uusimpaan tekniikkaan vaikka juhlavampia syntymäpäiviä tai isompaa kokousta suunniteleville.

Kino Tapiola on vuoden 2013 alusta kuulunut arvostettuun Europa Cinemas -verkostoon. Verkoston tavoitteena on tukea eurooppalaista elokuvakulttuuria ja eurooppalaisen elokuvan näkyvyyttä yleisön keskuudessa. Kino Tapiola on yksi kuudesta suomalaisesta verkostoon kuuluvasta elokuvateatterista. Asiakkaalle Europa Cinemas -sertifikaatti on tae teatterin runsaasta ja laadukkaasta eurooppalaisen elokuvan tarjonnasta.

Kino Tapiolan toiminnasta huolehtii Espoon elokuvajuhlat, eli samat ihmiset, jotka organisoivat vuosittain elokuussa järjestettävän Espoo Ciné -elokuvafestivaalin.

Description:
340 W. 10th St.
P.O. Box 1367
Indianapolis IN 46206

Several Hospitals and Health Centers. Enterprise potential.

Clarian was named among the "Best Hospitals in America" by USNews & World Report for five consecutive years, Clarian Health is the result of a cooperative effort of three downtown Indianapolis hospitals to combine resources, research, staff and administrative procedures.


Methodist Hospital, Indiana University Hospital and Riley Hospital for Children â€” all preeminent, state-of-the-art hospitals with long and distinguished histories â€” united in 1997 under Clarian Health. Clarian Health then opened Clarian West Medical Center in 2004, followed by Clarian North Medical Center in 2005, and Lafayette's Clarian Arnett Hospital in Fall 2008.


A private, nonprofit organization, Clarian owns or is affiliated more than 20 hospitals and health centers throughout Indiana and is closely tied to the Indiana University School of Medicine â€” more than half of the IU School of Medicine's 970 physicians practice, supervise, and teach residents and fellows at one of Clarian's hospitals. There are tens of thousand's, of doctors that are affiliated with Clarian Health Network.


You can learn more by logging onto www.clarian.org


On Friday May 29th, one of our staffing supervisors and I were able to job shadow several of the call center agents. I wanted to share some of the information that we gathered during that time in an effort to help you better understand Greg's needs and his facility.


There are three different types agents in Greg's Call Center that we were able to job shadow. They have triage calls that are answered by nurses.


1. Clarian On Call - This is were all agents start. They take calls to schedule appointments and to refer doctors within Clarian Health Network.
They are also currently making outbound calls to follow up on patients that have recently made a visit to the ER at the Clarian West Hospital. They found that patients were basically using the ER at that location for their primary care. There goal is to make recommendations for a Clarian Primary Care Physician to generate revenue


2. - IMAX - These agents take doctor to doctor calls and support the Clarian on Call Agents when needed. They take 50 to 100 calls per day.
Important skills for this position is spelling, quick retention of information, names and affiliations, knowledge of where to search the computer for pertinent information and good memory skills.


3. Switchboard - This is the highest stress level of all of the agents.
They take calls that vary from giving directions, paging doctors to Code Blue - Level One calls, which consist of contacting and connecting all of the doctors and agencies together, in the heat of an emergency such as a massive heart attack or organ transplant.


In all three positions medical terminology knowledge is very important!

The company also provides electronics, cameras and supplies, photo processing services, cellular phones, cellular service plan contracts and prepaid service, movies, music, video games, and books; stationery, automotive, hardware and paint, sporting goods, and outdoor living and horticulture, as well as fabrics, crafts, and seasonal merchandise; apparel for women, girls, men, boys, and infants, as well as shoes, jewelry, and accessories; and home furnishings, housewares and small appliances, bedding, home decor, and toys. In addition, it offers fuel and financial services and related products, including money orders, prepaid cards, wire transfers, money transfers, check cashing, and bill payment. In addition, it offers brand name merchandise, including hardgoods, softgoods, and selected private-label items, such as Members Mark. It operates 11,593 stores under 63 banners in 28 countries and e-commerce Websites in 11 countries. Wal-Mart Stores, Inc. was founded in 1945 and is headquartered in Bentonville.

Name: Sempra
Description: Sempra Energy's is an energy infrastructure company. The energy company operates electric and natural gas utilities in San Diego and Southern California, clean energy plants across the U.S., and energy distribution systems.

Name: First American
Description:
At First American our philosophy is simple. We are customer-centered experts in the title and escrow industry and are proud to be the largest independent title and escrow agent in Idaho and Montana. We currently serve 66 counties in Idaho and Montana with each of our offices employing local people. In addition to our local focus, we are dedicated to being a regional solution for settlement services. Our expert sales staff, coupled with state of the art technology, allows us to create a suite of services tailored to your specific needs. Finally, in order to effectively serve our customer's national needs, we operate as a seamless extension of our Underwriters: First American Title Insurance Company, Old Republic National Title Insurance Company, Commonwealth Land Title Insurance Company, Fidelity National Title Insurance Company, and Westcor Land Title Insurance Company. 

Thank you for choosing First American, we value your business and look forward to serving you. 

We Make Things Simple.

Name: PetSmart
Description: PetSmart, Inc. is the largest specialty pet retailer of services and solutions for the lifetime needs of pets. At PetSmart, we love pets, and we believe pets make us better people. Every day with every connection, PetSmart's passionate associates help bring pet parents closer to their pets so they, together, can live more fulfilled lives. This vision impacts everything we do for our customers, the way we support our associates and how we give back to our communities. PetSmart employs approximately 55,000 associates and operates more than 1,600 pet stores in the United States, Canada and Puerto Rico, as well as more than 200 in-store PetSmart Pets HotelÂ® dog and cat boarding facilities. The retailer provides a broad range of competitively priced pet food and products, as well as services such as dog training, pet grooming, pet boarding, PetSmart Doggie Day Campâ„¢ and pet adoption. PetSmart, PetSmart CharitiesÂ® and PetSmart CharitiesÂ® of Canada work with more than 3,500 animal welfare organizations to bring adoptable pets into stores so they have the best chance possible of finding a forever home. Through this in-store adoption program and other signature events, PetSmart has facilitated more than 8 million adoptions, more than any other brick-and-mortar organization. In May 2017, PetSmart acquired Chewy.com, a leading online retailer of pet food and products in the U.S., which operates as an independent subsidiary.

Name: Abbott Laboratories
Description: Abbott Laboratories manufactures and sells health care products worldwide. The companys Established Pharmaceutical Products segment offers branded generic pharmaceuticals to treat pancreatic exocrine insufficiency; irritable bowel syndrome or biliary spasm; intrahepatic cholestasis or depressive symptoms; gynecological disorders; hormone replacement therapy; dyslipidemia; hypertension; hypothyroidism; MÃ©nire's disease and vestibular vertigo; pain, fever, and inflammation; migraines; anti-infective clarithromycin; cardiovascular and metabolic products; and influenza vaccines, as well as to regulate physiological rhythm of the colon. Its Diagnostic Products segment provides immunoassay and clinical chemistry systems; assays used to screen and/or diagnosis cancer, cardiac, drugs of abuse, fertility, infectious diseases, and therapeutic drug monitoring; hematology systems and reagents; diagnostic systems and cartridges; instruments to automate the extraction, purification, and preparation of DNA and RNA from patient samples, and detects and measures infectious agents; genomic-based tests; informatics and automation solutions; and a suite of informatics tools and professional services. The companys Nutritional Products segment provides pediatric and adult nutritional products. Its Vascular Products segment offers coronary, endovascular, vessel closure, and structural heart devices to treat vascular disease. The company also provides blood and flash glucose monitoring systems, including test strips, sensors, data management decision software, and accessories for people with diabetes; and medical devices for the eye, such as cataract and LASIK surgery, contact lens care, and dry eye products. It serves retailers, wholesalers, hospitals, health care facilities, laboratories, physicians' offices, and government agencies. The company was founded in 1888 and is headquartered in North Chicago.

Name: NutriSystem, Inc.
Description: Founded in 1972, Nutrisystem is a commercial weight-loss and management system that delivers frozen and pre-packaged meals direct to consumer. They are headquartered in Fort Washington, Pennsylvania.

Name: Health Markets
Description: HealthMarkets lets the self-employed shop for better insurance. The holding company offers health insurance through its MEGA Life and Health Insurance, Mid-West National Life Insurance Company of Tennessee, Chesapeake Life Insurance Company, and HealthMarkets Insurance to mostly self-employed individuals in the US. Health care options include PPOs, high-deductible plans, and health spending accounts (HSAs). HealthMarkets also provides supplemental dental, vision, accident, illness, and hospital indemnity insurance. The company manages a network of insurance brokers through its Insphere Insurance Solutions division. A consortium led by the Blackstone Group owns HealthMarkets.

Name: Penske Corporation
Description: Penske, headed by race-car legend Roger Penske, appears to be on the right track as a diversified transportation firm. Penske is a partner with GE Equipment Management in Penske Truck Leasing, a commercial truck rental operation with more than 210,000 vehicles at some 1,000 locations. Penske owns more than a third of publicly-traded auto dealer Penske Automotive Group (formerly United Auto Group), which runs 330-plus franchised dealerships in some 20 states, Puerto Rico, and the UK. Through Penske Motor it sells cars in California. Truck-Lite makes safety lights for boats, buses, cars, commercial trucks, construction equipment, and recreational vehicles. Roger Penske is the company's majority owner.

Name: Quill
Description: Despite its old-fashioned name, Quill Corporation is an Internet Age supplier of office supplies and equipment. Through Quill.com, the company distributes products to more than 1 million small and midsized US businesses via its website and catalog. To compete with superstores, the company markets some 160,000 products under the Quill brand, as well as under the Bic, Hewlett-Packard, Mead, Papermate, and Xerox names. Quill also equips medical professionals with workplace goods through its Quill Healthcare unit. Founded by Jack Miller in 1956, Quill is owned by office retailer Staples, which counts Quill among its North American Commercial segment.

Name: MoneyGram
Description: MoneyGram International is a global payment services company. MoneyGram's convenient and reliable network includes retailers, international post offices and financial institutions.

Name: Amway
Description: Customers who want door-to-door, one-stop shopping the American Way turn to Amway Corp. Doing business as Amway Global, the North American affiliate of Amway International, part of Alticor, is an online retailer and direct seller of personal care products, health supplements, and household cleaners. Its offerings include cosmetics, skin care and hair care products, vitamins, as well as air and water purifiers, cleaners for laundry and surfaces, and cookware. The lineup is sold under the name brands Artistry, Satinique, Nutrilite, eSpring, Legacy of Clean, and iCook, among others. Once known as Quixtar, the company in 2009 adopted the Amway Global name to align with the international status of the Amway brand.

Name: American Electric Power Company
Description: American Electric Power (AEP) takes its slice of the US power pie out of Middle America. The holding company is one of the largest power generators and distributors in the US. AEP owns the nation's largest electricity transmission system, a network of almost 40,000 miles. It also has 230,000 miles of distribution lines. Its electric utilities have 5.3 million customers in 11 states and have about 38,000 MW of largely coal-fired generating capacity. AEP is a top wholesale energy company; it markets electricity in the US. Other operations include coal and bulk commodities barge transportation services.

Name: The Western and Southern Life Insurance Company
Description: Even if you consider Ohio to be Out West, Western & Southern Financial Group wants to be your insurance destination. Through its subsidiaries, the company offers a variety of life insurance products and annuities; accident and supplemental health coverage; mutual funds; and other investment management products and services. Western & Southern's financial services include mutual fund administration, trust services, financial advisory, and real estate development; it owns or manages some $60 billion in assets. The company is licensed in most states and the District of Columbia. Western & Southern Financial Group was founded in 1888 and is owned by a mutual holding company.

Name: Christian Broadcasting Network
Description: The Christian Broadcasting Network (CBN) is one of the leading producers of religious television programming in the country, offering news and entertainment shows with a spiritual message. Its centerpiece is The 700 Club, a daily show featuring a mix of news and commentary, interviews, feature stories, and Christian ministry co-hosted by CBN founder Pat Robertson. The company's programs are syndicated to broadcast and cable TV outlets that reach audiences around the world. CBN generates most of its revenue through ministry donations.  286M company

Name: Support.com
Description: Support.com wants to be a pillar of tech support. The company's cloud-based Nexus platform proactively identifies and repairs hardware and software problems, reducing the need for technical support staffing. It also specializes in phone and Web support for a wide variety of technology issues related to computer security, data recovery, networking, file management, and software installation. Support.com serves consumers and small businesses, with its offerings available through its website and through partners such as retailers, broadband providers, and anti-virus software providers. Nearly all sales come from customers in the Americas.

Name: Constellation Energy Group
Description: Constellation Energy Corporation generates and sells electricity in the United States. The company operates through five segments: Mid-Atlantic, Midwest, New York, ERCOT, and Other Power Regions. It sells natural gas, renewable energy, and other energy-related products and services. The company has 32,400 megawatts of generating capacity consisting of nuclear, wind, solar, natural gas, and hydroelectric assets. It serves distribution utilities; municipalities; cooperatives; and commercial, industrial, governmental, and residential customers. The company was incorporated in 2021 and is headquartered in Baltimore, Maryland. Constellation Energy Corporation was formerly a subsidiary of Exelon Corporation.

Name: Comdata Corporation
Description: Comdata helps those with a long way to go and a short time to get there. The former subsidiary of Ceridian is a payments processor and Mastercard issuer that mainly serves the trucking industry. It provides fleet charge cards and real-time reporting of transaction information. Comdata also offers payroll and logistical consulting services. Comdata Cards also allow for quick fuel-ups via its point-of-sale and pay-at-the-pump technologies, and can be used for repair charges, travel reimbursement, and regulatory compliance reporting. The company goes beyond the trucking industry to offer retail, loyalty, and other card services to multiple sectors. It was acquired by  FleetCor in 2014.

Name: Rackspace, Inc.
Description: Rackspace Hosting, Inc., is the open cloud company. The Company offers a diverse portfolio of cloud computing services, including public, dedicated and private cloud, and hybrid hosting. The Company is a global company, selling its services to business customers in more than 120 countries. The Company also has operations located in multiple cities across the United States, as well as in London, Amsterdam, Zurich, Hong Kong, and Sydney. As of December 31, 2012, it served more than 200,000 business customers, and managed more than 90,000 servers and 3.1 million email accounts. The Companyâ€™s Fanatical Support is designed to generate customer experience and is incorporated into all aspects of its hosting services. Effective March 28, 2013, the Company acquired Exceptional Cloud Services.

Name: Agora Marketing
Description: ThinkDirect Marketing Group, Inc., a direct marketing company, provides direct sales, call transfer, and lead generation services in the United States. It offers direct mail lead generation, Internet-based lead generation, IVR lead generation, predictive dialer, and turn-key marketing programs. The company's on-site project management services include project consultation and development; in-house product-specific training and scripting for telemarketing associates; list acquisition and customer profile analysis; creative development and production for lead generation and follow-up correspondence via direct mail, insert media, email, and online; and data capture and reporting. It offers services for lead generation/call transfer, product sales/liquidation, direct-to-consumer sales, order entry and fulfillment, survey data collection, and membership drives. The company allows consumers to access sweepstakes, discounts on magazine subscriptions, and third-party products and services. ThinkDirect Marketing Group, Inc. was formerly known as Agora Marketing Solutions, Inc. and changed its name in January 2010. The company was founded in 1984 and is based in Largo, Florida.

Name: Ceridian
Description: Founded in 1992, Ceridian is a software company specializing in human resources and employee services. The company offers a Cloud HCM software that brings together real-time payroll, HR, benefits, time reporting, talent, and workforce management solutions in order to promote efficiency. Ceridian is headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota.

Name: National Grid
Description: Founded in 1990 and headquartered in London, UK, National Grid is an electricity, natural gas, and clean energy delivery company. National Grid is transforming electricity and natural gas networks with energy solutions to reduce greenhouse gas emissions in the UK and the US.

Name: Air Canada
BillingStreet: Air Canada Centre, 7373 CÃ´te-Vertu Blvd. West
Description: Air Canada is Canada's largest airline and the largest provider of scheduled passenger services in the Canadian market, the Canada-U.S. transborder market and in the international market to and from Canada. The company is headquartered in Quebec, Canada.

Name: American International Group (AIG)
Description: Headquartered in New York, NY and founded in 1919, American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is an insurance company. AIG provides property casualty insurance, life insurance, retirement products, mortgage insurance and other financial services operating through commercial insurance, consumer insurance, and corporate insurance.

Name: AIG Hawaii Insurance Company, Inc.
BillingStreet: 500 Ala Moana Boulevard Six Waterfront Plaza Third Floor
Description: Farmers Insurance Hawaii, Inc. underwrites personal insurance products and services in Hawaii. It offers auto, homeowners, life, motorcycle, and business insurance products. The company was formerly known as AIG Hawaii Insurance Company, Inc. and changed its name to Farmers Insurance Hawaii, Inc. in January 2010. The company was incorporated in 1984 and is based in Honolulu, Hawaii. As of July 1, 2009, Farmers Insurance Hawaii, Inc. operates as a subsidiary of Farmers Group, Inc.

Name: Manulife Financial Corporation
Description: Founded in 1887, Manulife Financial together with its subsidiaries, provides financial advice, insurance, and wealth and asset management solutions for individuals, groups, and institutions in Asia, Canada, and the United States. The company offers individual life, and individual and group long-term care insurance; and guaranteed and partially guaranteed annuity products through insurance agents, brokers, banks, financial planners, and direct marketing. The company also manages timberland and agricultural portfolios; and engages in insurance agency, portfolio and mutual fund management, mutual fund dealer, life and financial reinsurance, and mutual funds marketing businesses. The company is headquartered in Toronto, Ontario, Canada.

Name: Frontier Communications Corporation
Description: Monica Lester

Name: Fannie Mae
Description: The Federal National Mortgage Association, or Fannie Mae, has helped more than 50 million low- to middle-income families realize the American Dream. Like its brother Freddie Mac, the government-supported enterprise (GSE) provides liquidity in the mortgage market by buying mortgages from lenders and packaging them for resale, transferring risk from lenders and allowing them to offer mortgages to those who may not otherwise qualify. It owns or guarantees about $10.7 trillion in home loans, or more than a quarter of all outstanding mortgages in the US. Due to losses caused largely by the subprime mortgage crisis, the government seized both Fannie and Freddie in 2008. It plans to wind the GSEs down over time.

Name: Dick's Sporting Goods
Description: Founded in 1948, DICK'S Sporting Goods, Inc is an American sporting goods retail company, based in Coraopolis, Pennsylvania.

Name: New York Life Insurance Company
Description: An industry behemoth, New York Life Insurance set company records in its core life insurance and annuity businesses in 2016. It also managed to raise its surplus and asset valuation reserve to a record $23.2 billion despite a substantial dividend payout ($1.8 billion) and low interest rates that challenged the entire industry.

Name: Aramark
Description: Established in 1959, Aramark is a professional services company providing food services, facilities management, and uniform & career apparel to health care institutions, universities, school districts, stadiums, arenas and other businesses. The company is headquartered in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania & was founded in 1932.

Name: Advanced Call Center Technologies LLC
Description: Advanced Call Center Technologies, LLC provides contact center and back office support services to companies in financial services, communications, and technology industries. The company provides customer management services, including order processing, upsell and cross selling, lead generation, new customer acquisition, information request, and account services; receivables management services, such as automated contact options, letter series/postcards, bi-lingual recovery, skip tracing, credit bureau reporting, and automated follow-up; Spanish language services; and technical support services, such as online customer care and self service support, Email and chat response, internal service desk, and service order and up sell. It offers back office support services for claims, payment processing, fraud/dispute resolution, account reconciliation, correspondence management, and accounts payable. The company was founded in 1996 and is headquartered in Berwyn, Pennsylvania.

Name: Canadian Tire
Description:
Canadian Tire Corporation, Limited comprises two main business operations that offer a range of retail goods and services, including general merchandise, clothing, sporting goods, petroleum and financial services. It operates in two segments: Retail and Financial Services. It offers a range of products and services through a portfolio of the retail brands and banners in Canada, including Canadian Tire, MarkÂ’s Work Wearhouse, Sport Chek and Sports Experts. Its Canadian Tire branded retail business includes its automotive business line (including PartSource and Canadian Tire Petroleum) and its Living, Fixing and Playing business lines. The Financial Services segment consists of Canadian Tire Financial Services Limited and Canadian Tire Bank. Financial Services markets a range of Canadian Tire-branded credit cards, Canadian Tire Options MasterCard, the Cash Advantage MasterCard and the Gas Advantage MasterCard. On August 18, 2011, it acquired The Forzani Group Ltd.

From Merged Record (12/16/15):
Don't be fooled by its name: Canadian Tire sells much more than tires. About 490 Canadian Tire general merchandise stores run by a network of associate dealers across Canada sell automotive, home, and sports and leisure products, including bicycles. The company's 85-plus PartSource auto parts stores cater to automotive do-it-yourselfers and professionals, while its roughly 385-location Mark's Work Wearhouse chain offers work and casual apparel and footwear for men and women. In addition, its Canadian Tire Petroleum subsidiary is one of the country's largest independent gasoline retailers. Established in 1922, Canadian Tire in 2011 acquired Canada's largest sporting goods retailer, The Forzani Group

Name: Liberty Mutual Group
Description: Liberty Mutual Holding defends our freedom to buy car insurance. As the parent company of Liberty Mutual Group and its operating subsidiaries, Liberty Mutual is one of the top property/casualty insurers in the US and among the top 10 providers of automobile insurance. The company also offers homeowners' insurance, workers compensation, general liability, group disability, fire and surety, and commercial lines for small to large companies. Liberty Mutual operates through four business divisions: Personal Insurance, Commercial Insurance, Liberty International, and Global Specialty. It distributes its products through a diversified blend of independent and exclusive agents, brokers, and direct sales.

Name: The Bank of New York Mellon
Description: The Bank of New York Mellon Corporation provides financial products and services to institutions, corporations, and high net worth individuals in the United States and internationally. It operates through two segments, Investment Management and Investment Services. The company offers investment management; trust and custody; foreign exchange; fund administration; global collateral services; securities lending; depositary receipts; corporate trust; global payment/cash management; banking services; and clearing services. It also provides mutual funds, separate accounts, wealth management, and private banking services; and broker-dealer services and registered investment advisory services. In addition, the company is involved in credit-related activities, business exits, leasing operations, corporate treasury activities, and derivatives and other trading, as well as corporate and bank-owned life insurance and renewable energy investments activities. Further, it provides global markets services. The Bank of New York Mellon Corporation was founded in 1784 and is headquartered in New York.

Name: Sirius XM Radio
Description: Communication services, nec, nsk

Name: Chubb
Description: Here's the skinny on Chubb: The insurer is best known for comprehensive personal homeowners insurance for the demographic that owns yachts (the company insures those, too). Chubb also offers commercial property/casualty insurance including multiple peril, property and marine, and workers' compensation. Its specialty insurance arm offers professional liability policies for executives across a spectrum of industries and also provides construction and commercial surety bonds. Chubb distributes its products through 8,000 independent agents and brokers in 120 offices across the US and in more than 25 countries. Swiss insurer  ACE is buying Chubb in a $28.3 billion transaction.

Name: John Hancock Financial
Description: John Hancock Life Insurance is an insurance company based in Boston, MA. In addition to their life insurance offerings, John Hancock provides financial services for annuities, COIN, college savings, group annuities, investments, retirement, rollover, safe-access accounts, and travel insurance. In 2004, John Hancock was acquired by the Canadian life insurance company Manulife Financial.

Name: W.W. Grainger (aka Grainger)
Description:
Notes from opportunities:

other opp: 60 agents
